_instructions.pdf_support: Place a PDF as 'slides.pdf' in the slides folder for embedded PDF viewer

title: Mouse Basics
subtitle: Click, Double Click, Drag and Explore!
overview: Students learn how to use a computer mouse by practicing clicking, double clicking, right clicking and dragging. They connect mouse actions with real-life examples like ATM machines, games and school computers.

images._note: Add photos from actual computer lab sessions and students using the mouse.

gallery.caption: Computer mouse helps control the cursor on screen
Left click is used to select items

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Student opening folder using double click
gallery.caption: Double click quickly opens files and folders

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of right click menu
gallery.caption: Right click opens extra options menu

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Student dragging object on screen
gallery.caption: Drag and drop helps move objects on screen
Students practicing mouse control in computer lab

keyTopics: Mouse controls the cursor on the computer screen
Left Click — Select objects and buttons
Double Click — Open apps, games and folders
Right Click — Show extra menu options
Drag and Drop — Move items from one place to another
Scroll Wheel — Move page up and down
Cursor movement — Smooth hand movement and control
Real-life use — ATM machines, games, drawing apps

youtubeVideos.title: Mouse Basics for Beginners
youtubeVideos.desc: Simple explanation of mouse buttons and cursor movement for kids.

youtubeVideos.title: How to Use a Computer Mouse
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn clicking, dragging and scrolling in a fun way.

activity.title: Mouse Click Practice Activity
steps: Move the cursor around the screen
Right click to see menu options
Drag and drop shapes or pictures
Scroll page up and down using wheel

quiz.q: Which device helps move the cursor on the screen?
quiz.explanation: Mouse helps control the cursor on the screen.

quiz.q: Which mouse button is mostly used for selecting items?
quiz.explanation: Left click is mainly used for selecting objects.

quiz.q: What does a double click do?
quiz.explanation: Double clicking quickly opens apps, folders and files.

quiz.q: What happens when we right click?
quiz.explanation: Right click shows additional menu options.
Dragging means holding and moving objects on screen.

quiz.q: Which part of the mouse helps scroll pages?
quiz.explanation: Scroll wheel helps move pages up and down.
Mouse gives commands to the computer, so it is an input device.

quiz.q: Which action is used to move files on screen?
quiz.explanation: Drag and drop helps move files and objects.

quiz.q: Which real-life machine uses cursor selection like a computer?
quiz.explanation: ATM machines use screen selection similar to computers.

quiz.q: What should students practice for better mouse control?
quiz.explanation: Careful movement improves mouse skills and accuracy.
